scenarios.name: hs-basic-get
scenarios.behavior: GET a live route returns 200 with body

scenarios.name: hs-head-no-body
scenarios.behavior: HEAD returns headers with no message body (curl_compliance)
scenarios.note: deployed serve does not strip the entity body on HEAD (returns the GET body, Content-Length-consistent) — HEAD specialisation not wired [ledger h1.1]

scenarios.name: hs-keepalive
scenarios.behavior: two sequential GETs on one keep-alive connection both answered

scenarios.name: hs-connection-close
scenarios.behavior: Connection: close honoured

scenarios.name: hs-http10
scenarios.behavior: HTTP/1.0 request served

scenarios.name: hs-path-traversal
scenarios.behavior: dot-dot path traversal does not serve a file outside the root

scenarios.name: hs-large-headers
scenarios.behavior: a large but legal header block is accepted

scenarios.name: smug-cl-te
scenarios.behavior: CL+TE conflict does not desync (front-CL/back-TE class)
scenarios.note: no desync; deployed shell frames on Content-Length (rejects/routes residue, no spec-400) — ledger h1.5 PARTIAL

scenarios.name: smug-te-cl
scenarios.behavior: TE+CL conflict does not desync (front-TE/back-CL class)
scenarios.note: no desync; deployed shell frames on Content-Length (rejects/routes residue, no spec-400) — ledger h1.5 PARTIAL

scenarios.name: smug-dup-cl
scenarios.behavior: duplicate Content-Length is not accepted as a smuggling vector
scenarios.note: shell routes to 404 rather than the reference's strict 400 (ledger h1.5)

scenarios.name: smug-neg-cl
scenarios.behavior: negative Content-Length rejected/neutralised
scenarios.note: shell routes to 404 rather than the reference's strict 400 (ledger h1.5)

scenarios.name: smug-chunked-bad-size
scenarios.behavior: invalid chunk size rejected
scenarios.note: hard parse-failure: connection closed (rejected)

scenarios.name: smug-te-not-last
scenarios.behavior: Transfer-Encoding whose final coding is not chunked is not smuggled
scenarios.note: shell routes to 404 rather than the reference's strict 400 (ledger h1.5)

scenarios.name: smug-crlf-injection
scenarios.behavior: CRLF in the request target does not inject a header/second request
scenarios.note: shell routes to 404 rather than the reference's strict 400 (ledger h1.5)

scenarios.name: smug-bad-request-line
scenarios.behavior: request line without an HTTP version is rejected/neutralised
scenarios.note: shell routes to 404 rather than the reference's strict 400 (ledger h1.5)

scenarios.name: smug-header-no-colon
scenarios.behavior: header field without a colon is rejected/neutralised
scenarios.note: shell routes to 404 rather than the reference's strict 400 (ledger h1.5)

scenarios.name: proxy-forward
scenarios.behavior: reverse proxy forwards to a live upstream

scenarios.name: proxy-hop-by-hop
scenarios.behavior: hop-by-hop headers named in Connection are stripped before the upstream

scenarios.name: proxy-roundrobin
scenarios.behavior: requests load-balance across >=2 live upstreams

scenarios.name: cache-hit
scenarios.behavior: two identical cacheable GETs: the second is served from cache

scenarios.name: cache-coalesce
scenarios.behavior: 8 concurrent identical cacheable GETs are coalesced (single-flight)

scenarios.name: cache-no-store-unsafe
scenarios.behavior: an unsafe method is not served from / does not poison the cache

scenarios.name: h2spec-engine-keepalive
scenarios.behavior: h2spec full suite vs the verified H2 engine (keep-alive host, h2c)
scenarios.note: verified engine via conformance host (h2c-host); the 1 skip is a TLS-only check, N/A on cleartext

scenarios.name: h2spec-deployed-tls-keepalive
scenarios.behavior: h2spec full suite vs the DEPLOYED dataplane (TLS h2, Tls.h2Loop keep-alive)
scenarios.note: the deployed dataplane DOES keep h2 alive over TLS via the verified Tls.h2Loop; residual failures are dynamic SETTINGS_INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE re-pacing (flow-control window resize)

scenarios.name: h2spec-deployed-h2c-oneshot
scenarios.behavior: h2spec full suite vs the DEPLOYED dataplane cleartext h2c (one-shot shell)
scenarios.note: the deployed cleartext h2c shell (crates/dataplane/src/blocking.rs h2c prior-knowledge path) is ONE-SHOT: it collects the opening burst, serves once, and closes; multi-flight tests (SETTINGS sync, PING, WINDOW_UPDATE pacing) cannot proceed. Keep-alive h2 IS wired over TLS (see h2spec-deployed-tls-keepalive). Gap: a keep-alive cleartext h2c IO shell.

scenarios.name: mesh-disco-natt
scenarios.behavior: DISCO NAT-traversal ping/pong/call-me-maybe reaches the verified FSM state

scenarios.name: mesh-derp-relay
scenarios.behavior: DERP relay server forwards a frame A->relay->B end-to-end

scenarios.name: mesh-wireguard-kernel
scenarios.behavior: WireGuard tunnel interoperates with the Linux kernel wg (both directions)
scenarios.note: driven live by the wg-live rig against kernel wg; not self-contained here

scenarios.name: mesh-derp-client-derper
scenarios.behavior: DERP client handshakes against a real derper relay
scenarios.note: driven live by derp-live against a real derper; not self-contained here

scenarios.name: cat-h3-qpack-dynamic
scenarios.behavior: QPACK dynamic table insert/lookup/evict + encoder/decoder instruction streams
scenarios.note: ledger h3.3 PARTIAL: dyn decode proven; full encoder-stream dynamic table partial

scenarios.name: cat-h3-early-data-425
scenarios.behavior: H3 0-RTT early-data method-safety + 425 Too-Early
scenarios.note: ledger h3.6 PARTIAL: quic-layer replay proven; h3 anti-replay adapter partial

scenarios.name: cat-h3-live-h3spec
scenarios.behavior: H3 frame/SETTINGS/GOAWAY conversation vs an external H3 client
scenarios.note: driven by conformance/h3_client.py + aioquic in the base suite, not here

scenarios.name: cat-dns-record-types
scenarios.behavior: MX/NS/SOA/CNAME/TXT/SRV/PTR resolution vs a live resolver
scenarios.note: driven by conformance/dns/battery.py (dig, 56/56) in the DNS rig

scenarios.name: cat-dns-svcb-https
scenarios.behavior: SVCB/HTTPS record (type 64/65) ALPN + port hints
scenarios.note: proven dn.10 HAVE-PROVEN; driven by the DNS rig, not this runner

scenarios.name: cat-dns-naptr-tlsa
scenarios.behavior: NAPTR + TLSA (DANE) record resolution
scenarios.note: ledger dn.7/dn.8 MODEL-ONLY: record model exists, no resolver path

scenarios.name: cat-dns-system-fallback
scenarios.behavior: system-resolver fallback (getaddrinfo side thread)
scenarios.note: ledger dn.15 MISSING

scenarios.name: cat-control-registration
scenarios.behavior: machine registration (interactive + pre-auth-key) + expiry
scenarios.note: proven cp.3 HAVE-PROVEN; needs a headscale-style peer, not self-contained here

scenarios.name: cat-control-netmap-delta
scenarios.behavior: long-poll netmap stream: full snapshot + incremental deltas
scenarios.note: proven cp.4 HAVE-PROVEN; needs a coordination-server peer

scenarios.name: cat-control-local-daemon
scenarios.behavior: local-daemon API (status/whois/serve-config/IPN bus)
scenarios.note: ledger cp.10 MISSING

scenarios.name: cat-control-funnel
scenarios.behavior: serve-config push / Funnel (expose a service publicly)
scenarios.note: ledger cp.11 MISSING

scenarios.name: cat-transport-cq-timers
scenarios.behavior: kernel-native + linked timeouts, unified deadline min-heap
scenarios.note: ledger cq.5 PARTIAL / cq.6 MISSING (proptest_timers)

scenarios.name: cat-transport-multibackend
scenarios.behavior: multi-backend reactor (epoll/kqueue/IOCP/poll) + io_uring->readiness fallback
scenarios.note: ledger cq.2 PARTIAL / cq.3 MISSING: models exist, deployed = io_uring+blocking

scenarios.name: cat-transport-turn
scenarios.behavior: TURN allocation client/engine + shared-socket STUN/QUIC/data demux
scenarios.note: ledger st.3/st.4 MISSING

scenarios.name: cat-transport-udp-perf
scenarios.behavior: GSO/GRO batched UDP, ECN + MTU discovery, AF_XDP kernel-bypass
scenarios.note: ledger qu.10/11/12 MISSING (QUIC throughput paths)

scenarios.name: cat-mesh-netstack-tun
scenarios.behavior: kernel TUN device + userspace netstack (TCP/IP over the tunnel)
scenarios.note: ledger me.6/me.7 MISSING (blocks real mesh service fronting)

scenarios.name: cat-proxy-connect-tunnel
scenarios.behavior: CONNECT tunnel (blind) + CONNECT MITM intercept
scenarios.note: ledger conn.1/conn.2 MISSING (forward-proxy CONNECT cluster)

scenarios.name: cat-proxy-grpc
scenarios.behavior: gRPC proxy + gRPC-Web bridge + gRPC health-check proxy
scenarios.note: ledger px.15/16/17 MISSING

scenarios.name: cat-client-session
scenarios.behavior: first-class HTTP client: connection reuse, retry budget, redirect-follow
scenarios.note: ledger cl.1 PARTIAL / cl.4 MISSING (blocks ACME fetch, health probes)

scenarios.name: cat-pki-ocsp-stapling
scenarios.behavior: OCSP stapling with background refresh
scenarios.note: ledger pk.11 PARTIAL: OCSP verify proven; stapling refresh job partial

scenarios.name: cat-pki-proxy-protocol
scenarios.behavior: PROXY protocol v1/v2 real-client-addr recovery
scenarios.note: ledger pk.18 MISSING

scenarios.name: cat-lifecycle-zero-downtime
scenarios.behavior: zero-downtime binary upgrade (fd inheritance, conn/TLS-state hand-off)
scenarios.note: ledger lc.6 MISSING
